title: "Tri-Boot(What should i put in for ubuntu?)"

### Post by Linux_Noobie on 2006-09-01
Dual boot.
Im trying to dual boot windows-linspire-and ubuntu and i have them all installed on they're partition on the drive but now i need to know how to add it to my menu.lst In linspire the acctual menu.lst file is
```
# Generated by jiffyboot version 8.172. If this file is edited, the
# system will stop modifying it.  To allow the system to resume
# management of this file, remove it and run /sbin/jiffyboot

default=0
timeout=0
fallback 

title hidden
configfile (hd0,3)/boot/grub/menu-normal.lst

```
And that just refer's to menu-normal.lst so i thought it might be needed.
```
# Generated by jiffyboot version 8.172. If this file is edited, the
# system will stop modifying it.  To allow the system to resume
# management of this file, remove it and run /sbin/jiffyboot

default=0
timeout=10
color cyan/green magenta/green
splashimage=/boot/grub/bootsplash.xpm.gz

instructions Use the %c and %c keys to select which entry is highlighted.
timeout_message Entry %d will be booted automatically in %d seconds.

# hda4, boot entry
title Linspire 5.1.427
root (hd0,3)
kernel /boot/vmlinuz-2.6.14  root=/dev/ide/host0/bus0/target0/lun0/part4 rootdev=0x0304 ramdisk=33792 video=vesafb:nomtrr jiffymount=noatime resume2=swap:/dev/hda4:0x44000 vga=0x311 splash=silent
initrd /boot/initrd-2.6.14.gz

# hda4, diagnostic boot entry of /dev/ide/host0/bus0/target0/lun0/part4
title Diagnostics
root (hd0,3)
kernel /boot/vmlinuz-2.6.14 noresume2 single Diagnostics splash=0  root=/dev/ide/host0/bus0/target0/lun0/part4 rootdev=0x0304 ramdisk=33792 video=vesafb:nomtrr jiffymount=noatime resume2=swap:/dev/hda4:0x44000
initrd /boot/initrd-2.6.14.gz

# hda4, redetect boot entry of /dev/ide/host0/bus0/target0/lun0/part4
title Redetect
root (hd0,3)
kernel /boot/vmlinuz-2.6.14 noresume2 redetect splash=0  root=/dev/ide/host0/bus0/target0/lun0/part4 rootdev=0x0304 ramdisk=33792 video=vesafb:nomtrr jiffymount=noatime resume2=swap:/dev/hda4:0x44000
initrd /boot/initrd-2.6.14.gz

# hda1, MS Windows
title Microsoft® Windows® 
root (hd0,0)
chainloader +1

title Advanced Menu
configfile (hd0,3)/boot/grub/advanced-normal.lst
```
So because i have Ubuntu on hda2 i thought i should just add this.
```
# hda2 , Ubuntu
title Ubuntu
root (hd0,1)
kernel  /boot/vmlinuz-2.6.12-9-386 root=/dev/hda2 ro quiet splash
initrd  /boot/initrd.img-2.6.12-9-386
savedefault
boot
```
But it doesnt boot :( I just get a error when trying. What did i do wrong??

### Post by easyease on 2006-09-30
hi, im not sure if will help but I am tri-booting on my pc windows xp/ubuntu breezy/ubuntu dapper, i found that if i installed breezy last of the three os from an install disc it auto detected the other operating systems on my system and added them to the breezy grub.
 Im only running both breezy and dapper because dapper doesnt recognise my usb dvb device.
